steps.title: Use the axis of symmetry
steps.content: The vertex is $(-2,5)$, so the axis of symmetry is $x=-2$. The two $x$-intercepts of a parabola are the same horizontal distance from this axis.

steps.title: Mirror the known root across the axis
steps.content:
Other intercept $= 2h - r_1 = 2(-2) - \left(-\dfrac{15}{4}\right) = -4 + \dfrac{15}{4} = -\dfrac{16}{4} + \dfrac{15}{4} = -\dfrac{1}{4}$.
The other x-intercept is $\left(-\dfrac{1}{4}, 0\right)$, so Choice B is the correct answer.
